title: Thaw cryopreserved cells
description: Universal routine: rapid 37 C thaw, dilute into prewarmed medium, recover. Sources: Gibco + ATCC.

payload.description: Universal protocol for recovering cryopreserved mammalian cells from liquid nitrogen, for adherent or suspension lines. Work quickly; thawing is stressful to frozen cells. Sources: Gibco Cell Culture Basics https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home/references/gibco-cell-culture-basics/cell-culture-protocols/thawing-cells.html ; ATCC Animal Cell Culture Guide https://www.atcc.org/-/media/resources/culture-guides/animal-cell-culture-guide.pdf

cell_line.name: Any mammalian cell line
cell_line.notes: Use the complete growth medium specific to your cell line, prewarmed to 37 C. Sources: Gibco and ATCC (see description).

media.base_medium: Complete growth medium for your cell line, prewarmed to 37 C

planned_events.notes: Thaw: remove the vial from liquid nitrogen and swirl in a 37 C water bath until just a sliver of ice remains (Gibco: under 1 min; ATCC: about 2 min). Wipe the vial with 70% ethanol. Transfer cells dropwise into 9 to 10 mL prewarmed complete medium. To remove cryoprotectant, centrifuge gently (Gibco: about 200 x g, 5 to 10 min; ATCC: 125 x g, 10 min), discard the supernatant, resuspend, and seed at high density. Lines that tolerate it can be diluted directly into a large volume of medium instead.
Examine cultures after 24 h (ATCC). For adherent cells, replace the medium once cells attach to remove residual DMSO. Subculture as needed.
First passage once cells are growing well and near the target confluence or density for your line.
